dossier.overview:
Starter skeleton for a new Resort Docs article. Replace every REPLACE_* placeholder, delete sections you do not need, and keep repo paths relative to each app root in the monorepo (pokemon-resort/, pokemon-resort-page/, spmk/, island-dreamforge/).

Save the file under public/docs/articles/{category}/{slug}.json where category matches the card in docs.json (meta, formats, gameplay, or design). Set apps[] on the card for hub filters. For a tabbed guide, add layout: "tabs-primary" on the section that holds your main tabs block.

sections.title: Summary
sections.summary: One paragraph lede for this section — what the reader will learn.

blocks.body: REPLACE_SUMMARY — plain-language explanation of the topic, audience, and where it lives in the repo.

sections.title: Core concepts
sections.summary: Definitions and mental model before file layouts or code.

blocks.caption: Optional — delete if not needed

sections.title: Implementation
sections.summary: Linked source files and tabbed write/read paths when a format has two sides.

blocks.body: Use code blocks to cite real files. repo is the app root folder name; path is relative to that root. Paste the snippet into body and keep lines in sync when the source changes.

blocks.caption: Primary runtime entry — REPLACE description
Tooling that writes or validates the same format
Use tabs when one topic has distinct write vs read paths (e.g. encoding vs decoding).
